title: Pain Point
description: One structured pain-point entry. Each entry answers who hurts, how badly, how many of them exist, what they pay today, why today's tools miss them, and whether a builder could plausibly reach $50,000 in monthly revenue. Inspired by ideabrowser.com, painonsocial.com, the Santifer repair-shop case study, and a16z / Menlo Ventures writing on vertical AI.

vertical.description: Industry label. For example: Device Repair, Veterinary, HVAC, Legal for small businesses.

workflow_family.description: One of seven kinds of operational work this pain falls under. 'full_lifecycle' covers Santifer-style end-to-end bundles.

persona.description: The specific role who feels the pain and would pay to fix it. Be specific. Not 'small businesses' but 'HVAC shop owner with 5 to 30 technicians'.

pain_description.description: Plain-language description of what breaks, written in the persona's voice.

pain_severity.description: How painful this is. 1 is a minor annoyance. 10 is losing money or triggering regulatory risk.

tedium_score.description: How repetitive the task is. 10 is pure copy-paste data entry. High tedium is the strongest signal for AI replacement.

frequency.description: How often the pain happens, in human terms. For example: 'dozens of times a day per shop'.

tam_firms.description: Estimated number of target companies in the market. Null if unknown. Used for bottom-up market sizing.

tam_labor_spend_usd.description: Total annual labor cost in this market, in US dollars. Null if unknown. Frames the pain as a labor-budget opportunity, not an IT-budget one.

tam_direction.description: Is this market getting bigger or smaller?

current_wtp_usd_month.description: What the target customer pays today, per account per month, to the nearest existing tool. In US dollars. Anchors your pricing ceiling.

incumbent_tools.description: Existing tools and SaaS products already serving this pain.

incumbent_gap.description: Why today's tools fail these customers. Too rigid, too expensive, not AI-native, fragmented across systems, etc.

santifer_pattern.description: One-line solution sketch. Typically: single source of truth (Airtable) + automation (n8n, Make) + AI agent + conversational channels (WhatsApp, voice).

evidence_count.description: How many independent sources (Reddit threads, G2 reviews, forum posts) back up this pain.

sources.description: URLs that back up the pain: Reddit threads, forum posts, G2 / Capterra reviews, case studies, industry reports.

verbatim_quotes.description: Real words from real users pulled from public Reddit, forum, or review threads. Quoted exactly as written. No paraphrasing.

quote.description: Exact text of the complaint. Use '...' to show you skipped words.

source_url.description: Direct link to the thread, review, or post.

source_label.description: Human-readable source name. For example: 'r/smallbusiness', 'G2 review', 'Rossmann Group forum'.

author_handle.description: Public handle of the author (Reddit username, reviewer name). Leave empty if the author is anonymous.

date.description: Post date in YYYY-MM or YYYY-MM-DD format if known.

reference_build.description: URL of an existing working example of this solution pattern, if one exists.

why_now.description: What changed recently that makes this buildable now. A cost crash (LLMs), a new API (WhatsApp Business), or a new regulation.

opportunity_score.description: Composite 0-100 score combining pain severity, tedium, market direction, and what customers pay today.

specific_prospects.description: Named, reachable people, companies, and communities you could contact today. Not 'repair shop owners' but 'Drew at iCare Repairs, Durham NC'. This is what makes an entry actionable rather than abstract.

name.description: The real name: a company, founder, subreddit, Discord server, or industry forum.

url.description: Direct URL to the profile, community, or website.

signal_size.description: How big this audience is. For example: '2.1M subscribers', '40 technicians, Series A', '314 complaints in 2024-2026'.

why_reachable.description: How you would actually reach them. Founder's public LinkedIn, subreddit where DMs are common, listed email address, industry conference attendee list.

velocity_signal.description: Is this pain growing, flat, or shrinking right now? A static market size doesn't tell you whether you're too early or too late. This field does.

direction.description: accelerating_fast = rapid growth. accelerating = steady growth. flat = stable. cooling = shrinking.

evidence.description: Concrete observation with numbers and dates. For example: 'r/priorauth subscribers grew from 8,000 to 26,000 between 2024 and 2026.'

trigger.description: What's causing the growth: a new API launch, a new regulation, a cost collapse, a platform unlock.

window_months.description: Estimated months of head start you have before mass competition enters this space.

freshness_date.description: Year-month (YYYY-MM) when this signal was last verified. Older than 6 months = stale.

buying_intent_evidence.description: Quotes and threads where someone actively signals they want a solution or will pay for one. Different from generic complaint evidence. 'What should I use?' is a buying signal. 'This sucks' is not.

intent_type.description: asking_recommendation = 'what should I use'. actively_evaluating = comparing specific tools. willing_to_pay_stated = explicit budget mention. churning_from_incumbent = switching away from a current tool. waitlist_signal = signed up for beta. funding_announcement = investor money flowing into the category.

kill_risks.description: Three to five things that could kill this idea. Each with a severity level and current status. If any 'kill' risk is already active, walk away.

risk.description: What specifically could kill this. An incumbent responding, a regulation changing, a platform shutting off an API, a core assumption being wrong.

severity.description: kill = if true, the idea is dead. serious = a real threat that requires mitigation. manageable = factor it in and move on.

current_status.description: Is this risk active right now, dormant, or resolved? Cite evidence when you can.

fermi_math_to_50k.description: The arithmetic path from zero to $50,000 in monthly revenue, written out in the open. If the math does not clear the bar, the entry should be cut. The goal is to force every entry to answer whether it can become a real business, not just a viable complaint.

reachable_som_firms.description: How many target companies you could realistically get in front of in year one. Not the full market, not the 'serviceable' market, just the real top of your funnel.

expected_conversion_pct.description: Plausible rate of trials that become paying customers. For cold B2B SaaS, 1 to 3 percent is realistic. For consumer, 0.3 to 1 percent.

anchor_price_usd_month.description: What you would charge per account per month, in US dollars. Anchor this to what existing tools charge or to the dollar value you save the customer.

paying_customers_for_50k.description: Simple division: $50,000 divided by your monthly price, rounded up.

months_to_50k_at_realistic_pace.description: Estimated months to hit $50,000 in monthly revenue at realistic conversion and growth rates.

verdict.description: clears_bar = the math works at conservative assumptions. tight_but_possible = the math works only with favorable assumptions. math_does_not_work = even with generous assumptions, you can't get there.

notes.description: Short explanation of how you got the numbers above and what has to go right.
